Softmax vs Linear Attention: Memory Growth

Each new token grows the Softmax attention matrix by one row + column (O(n²) total).
The Linear attention accumulator Ct stays fixed at d×d — only its values change.

Speed: n = 0
Softmax Attention
Attn(Q,K,V) = softmax(QKT/√d) · V
Memory: O(n²) — full n×n matrix
0 cells
Memory0 cells
Linear Attention
outt = φ(qt) · Ct
Ct = Ct−1 + φ(kt) vtT
Memory: O(d²) — fixed d×d accumulator
d² = 16 cells
Ct ∈ ℝd×d (d = 4, shown as heatmap)
Size is constant — only values update as tokens arrive
Memory16 cells
